hero.badge: Full-Stack Engineer · AI Developer · MCP Practitioner · Glasgow
hero.subheading: Full-stack engineer with 1+ year of advanced AI development — building agentic workflows, integrating MCP servers, and shipping production features through vibe coding. Java, TypeScript, and the entire AI-native stack.

aiActivities.title: Advanced AI Development
aiActivities.description: Over a year of hands-on experience building AI-augmented full-stack applications — integrating LLMs, prompt engineering, RAG pipelines, and AI-powered APIs into production-grade systems.

aiActivities.title: Vibe Coding
aiActivities.description: Pioneered a vibe-coding workflow using GitHub Copilot, Cursor, and Claude — dramatically accelerating development cycles. From rapid prototyping to production features, AI is embedded in every commit.

aiActivities.title: Advanced MCP Usage
aiActivities.description: Deep expertise in Model Context Protocol — building and consuming MCP servers to extend AI agents with real-world tools: databases, APIs, CI/CD pipelines, Figma, GitHub, and more.

aiActivities.title: Full-Stack AI Integration
aiActivities.description: Bridging back-end Java/Spring services with AI-native front-ends in Next.js. Shipping features with agentic code review, AI-generated tests, and automated documentation at every layer of the stack.

copilotCards.title: AI Code Completions
copilotCards.desc: Inline suggestions across every file — TypeScript, Java, CSS and more. Dramatically reduces boilerplate and accelerates feature delivery.

copilotCards.title: Copilot Chat
copilotCards.desc: Context-aware conversations about code, architecture decisions, debugging, and refactoring — right inside the editor without context switching.

copilotCards.title: Agentic Workflows
copilotCards.desc: Multi-step task automation using Copilot agents paired with MCP tools — scaffolding, code review, test generation and CI/CD orchestration.

blogPosts.title: Spring HATEOAS (Hypertext as the Engine of Application State)
blogPosts.excerpt: Spring HATEOAS provides REST API component to the client response shared with other network application meta-data information through processing the dynamic data — links to resources. HAL (Hypertext Application Language) is a format of resource representation that can be implemented in HATEOAS and also share the URI schema without breaking clients connectivity.
blogPosts.author: Arul

blogPosts.title: Microprofile 3.3 Micro-services using Liberty
blogPosts.excerpt: Exploring MicroProfile 3.3 to build cloud-native microservices using Open Liberty. This article covers service discovery, health checks, and fault tolerance patterns that make enterprise Java microservices production-ready.
blogPosts.author: Arul
